Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the mortgage LOS for the Estrada loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Estrada" (or similar), or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00701". Expected findings: FHA purchase loan for Rafael Estrada at 3847 Sardis Rd, Charlotte, NC 28270. Purchase price $285,000, loan amount $275,025, rate 6.375% locked on 2026-03-18 expiring 2026-05-02, 3.5% down payment, LTV 96.5%, DTI 44.2%, status "conditional_approval". Lender is UWM, assigned LO is Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), assigned processor is Sofia Reyes (los_staff_afc9caafae9d). Closing date 2026-05-08. Realtor is Maria Gonzalez (maria.gonzalez@icloud.com). Co-borrower is Maria Estrada. Borrower credit score 668, annual income $72,000, employer Novant Health.
OE 2: Retrieve conditions for the Estrada loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id for LN-2026-00701 (or similar). Expected findings: Two outstanding prior-to-closing conditions. Condition 1: Appraisal gap resolution required, appraised value $265,000 vs purchase price $285,000, borrower must bring additional funds or negotiate price reduction, status "outstanding", issued 2026-04-17. Condition 2: Homeowners insurance binder required, evidence of insurance must be provided to lender before closing, status "outstanding", issued 2026-03-20, binder from SouthPark Insurance pending.
OE 3: Retrieve the activity log for the Estrada loan using mortgage_los_get_activity_log with loan_id for LN-2026-00701 (or similar). Expected findings: Two notes from April 17. First by Sofia Reyes: appraisal received from Carolina Appraisal Group, value came in at $265,000, $20,000 below purchase price of $285,000. Second by Carlos Rivera: spoke with borrowers about appraisal gap, they do not have $20K in additional reserves, will look into ROV with better comps or price renegotiation with seller.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query "Estrada" or "Sardis" or "appraisal" or "LN-2026-00701" (or similar) to find email threads related to the Estrada file. Expected findings: Email from Michelle Tran at Carolina Appraisal Group (mtran@carolinaappraisal.com) to Sofia Reyes dated April 17 with the appraisal report for 3847 Sardis Rd showing appraised value $265,000, three comps used (3612 Sardis Rd $258,000, 4101 Sardis Rd $272,000, 3920 Monroe Rd $263,000), property backs up to creek with moderate erosion. CC'd to Carlos Rivera.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with query "insurance" or "binder" or "Sardis" or "flood" (or similar) to find the insurance email thread for the Estrada file. Expected findings: A 3-email chain between Tanya Morales at SouthPark Insurance (tmorales@southparkinsurance.com) and Sofia Reyes. (1) April 22: Tanya asks Sofia for the flood zone determination before issuing the binder. (2) April 23: Sofia replies with FEMA flood map info showing the property is in FEMA panel 3710578400J, rear portion of lot falls within Zone AE (1% annual chance floodplain) due to unnamed tributary of Four Mile Creek. (3) April 24: Tanya replies that because the property is in Zone AE, the borrowers will need separate NFIP flood insurance in addition to standard homeowners insurance. Breakdown: standard HOI $1,450/year, flood insurance (NFIP) $1,800/year, total $3,250/year ($270.83/month). The flood insurance alone adds approximately $150/month to the borrowers' monthly obligations. Tanya warns this may push DTI above the FHA maximum of 50%. This third email with the flood insurance breakdown is in Sofia's inbox and marked as unread (is_read = false). Carlos is not a recipient or CC on this thread, so he has no visibility into the flood insurance financial impact unless the agent reads this email chain or Sofia forwards it.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Estrada" or "insurance" or "binder" (or similar), or browse the DM channel between Sofia and Carlos (D_sofia_carlos) using conversations_history. Expected findings: Sofia's DM to Carlos on April 24 saying the insurance binder for the Estrada file is delayed, working on getting it sorted, should have an update Monday. Also Sofia's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 27 asking if anyone has dealt with Zone AE flood insurance requirement on an FHA file, mentioning it adds about $150/month and she is worried about DTI impact.
OE 7: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find realtor notes related to the Estrada deal. Expected findings: CRM engagement note dated April 22 from Maria Gonzalez regarding the Estrada purchase at 3847 Sardis Rd. The seller agent is getting very impatient with the appraisal gap situation. They have a backup offer from another buyer and are saying if the $20,000 gap is not resolved by Friday May 1 they will accept the backup offer and terminate the contract. Maria told Carlos he needs to either file the ROV immediately or get the Estradas to negotiate the price down. Title: "Estrada deal - seller threatening backup offer."
OE 8: Search the mortgage LOS for the Watkins loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Watkins" (or similar), or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00702". Expected findings: Conventional purchase loan for Angela Watkins at 6214 Fairview Rd, Charlotte, NC 28210. Purchase price $412,000, loan amount $370,800, 10% down payment, rate 6.625% locked 2026-03-13 expiring 2026-04-27 (expired yesterday), LTV 90.0%, DTI 38.5%, status "processing". Lender is AmeriHome (los_lender_533a73fed6b6), assigned LO is Carlos Rivera, assigned processor is Darnell Price (los_staff_0f9038c42665). Closing date 2026-05-22. Realtor is Derrick Coleman (derrick.coleman@icloud.com). Co-borrower Trevor Watkins. Borrower credit score 722, annual income $118,000, employer Novant Health - Presbyterian Medical Center.
OE 9: Retrieve conditions for the Watkins loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id for LN-2026-00702 (or similar). Expected findings: One outstanding prior-to-approval condition: Verbal Verification of Employment (VOE) required for Angela Watkins, employer Novant Health - Presbyterian Medical Center. Status "outstanding". Notes indicate three VOE attempts made (03/25, 04/07, 04/15), HR department at Novant Health unresponsive. File cannot be submitted to underwriting until VOE is completed.
OE 10: Check the rate lock status for the Watkins loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status with loan_id for LN-2026-00702 (or similar). Expected findings: Rate lock at 6.625% placed 2026-03-13 with AmeriHome, expired 2026-04-27 (yesterday). The lock has expired and the file is still in processing status, meaning it was never submitted to underwriting before the lock expired.
OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with query "Watkins" or "rate lock" or "LN-2026-00702" (or similar) to find the rate lock email chain. Expected findings: (1) April 20: Camille Foster warns Carlos the rate lock expires Monday April 27, lock was placed March 13 at 6.625% with AmeriHome for 45 days, file still in processing, extension fee is $375 for 7 days, needs authorization by end of day Thursday April 23. (2) April 21: Carlos replies saying he will talk to Darnell about the VOE and get back by end of week, trying to avoid the extension fee. (3) April 24: Camille escalates to Carlos CC Grace Yamamoto, saying she did not hear back, the lock expires Monday April 27, current rate for the same product is 7.0% (37.5 bps higher than 6.625%), increasing monthly payment by approximately $85. Post-expiration extension not guaranteed and costs $500 instead of $375.
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with query "AmeriHome" or "extension" or "SPRING2026" or "complimentary" (or similar) to discover the AmeriHome promotional email. Expected findings: Email dated April 23 from wholesale@amerihome.com to Darnell Price (darnell.price@keystonemortgage.com), CC'd to Sofia Reyes and Elena Marchetti, announcing a complimentary 7-day lock extension promotion. Effective through May 2, 2026, for all rate locks expiring between April 24 and May 2, 2026. Must be requested BEFORE the lock expiration date, through the AmeriHome broker portal under Lock Management, reference promo code SPRING2026. Discovery: This free extension would have covered the Watkins lock (which expired April 27, within the April 24 - May 2 window) but was never requested because the email went to Darnell, not Carlos or Camille. The opportunity is now missed since the lock already expired.
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with query "Angela Watkins" or "Fairview" or "closing timeline" (or similar) to find the borrower's email. Expected findings: Email from angela.watkins@outlook.com to Carlos dated April 27 (is_read = false), asking about the closing timeline for 6214 Fairview Rd, noting their lease ends May 31 and they were counting on closing by mid-May, the seller has been asking Derrick for updates and is getting anxious, requesting confirmation they are still on track for May 22 closing.
OE 14: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Watkins" or "rate lock" or "expir" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the Watkins file. Expected findings: (1) Camille's message in #rate-watch (C005) on April 23 listing rate locks expiring that week and next, flagging LN-2026-00702 (Watkins, conventional, AmeriHome) expires 4/27 and is still in processing, telling Carlos this needs immediate attention, also noting LN-2026-00701 (Estrada, FHA, UWM) expires 5/2 with appraisal gap unresolved. (2) Darnell's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 22 asking if anyone has a direct HR contact at Novant Health Presbyterian, noting he has been trying to complete a VOE for 5 weeks on the Watkins file (LN-2026-00702) and the main HR line just goes to voicemail after three attempts.
OE 15: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find realtor notes about the Watkins deal. Expected findings: CRM engagement note dated April 24 from Derrick Coleman regarding the Watkins purchase at 6214 Fairview Rd. The seller is reviewing a backup offer and needs a firm answer on closing timeline by end of day Wednesday April 30. The file has been in processing for over 6 weeks with no movement. This is his third time asking for an update this month. Title: "Watkins purchase - seller considering backup offers."
OE 16: Search the mortgage LOS for the Torres loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Torres" (or similar), or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00703". Expected findings: Conventional purchase loan for Priscilla Torres at 8401 Pineville-Matthews Rd, Charlotte, NC 28226. Purchase price $348,000, loan amount $313,200, 10% down payment, rate 6.75% locked 2026-04-01 expiring 2026-05-16, LTV 90.0%, DTI 36.8%, status "underwriting". Lender is Flagstar Wholesale (los_lender_fff8b2eea94d), assigned LO is Carlos Rivera, assigned processor is Sofia Reyes. Closing date 2026-05-15. Realtor is Brian Foster (brian.foster@mac.com). Co-borrower Jonathan Torres. Borrower credit score 735, annual income $104,000, employer Wells Fargo Corporate Office.
OE 17: Retrieve conditions for the Torres loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id for LN-2026-00703 (or similar). Expected findings: Four conditions. (1) Bank statements: "rejected" status, description says provide 2 months consecutive bank statements, only 1 month received. Notes reveal that the bank statement uploaded on 04/24 was rejected by underwriter because the name on the statement does not match borrower -- statement shows Patricia Lincoln, not Priscilla Torres. File suspended pending correct document. (2) IRS Form 4506-C: "cleared" status, cleared by Sofia on 04/23. (3) Pay stub: "cleared" status, cleared by Sofia on 04/23. (4) Letter of explanation for credit inquiry from Capital One Auto Finance dated 03/15/2026: "outstanding" status, borrower has not yet provided LOE.
OE 18: Search emails using search_emails with query "Torres" or "Flagstar" or "LN-2026-00703" or "suspended" (or similar) to find the Flagstar correspondence. Expected findings: (1) April 22: Flagstar Wholesale Underwriting (submissions@flagstar.com) emails Sofia issuing 4 conditions for LN-2026-00703 (bank statements, 4506-C, pay stub, LOE for Capital One credit inquiry). (2) April 24: Flagstar emails Sofia with subject "FILE SUSPENDED" -- the bank statement uploaded on 04/24 does not match the borrower, document shows Patricia Lincoln with Chase Bank account ending 7842 and balance $48,231.56, but borrower is Priscilla Torres. File suspended until correct documents received. This email is marked as unread (is_read = false). Discovery: This is the critical evidence that Sofia's "conditions are moving along fine" update to Carlos was misleading -- she either did not see or did not disclose the file suspension.
OE 19: Search emails using search_emails with query "Torres" or "Brian Foster" or "May 15" or "Pineville" (or similar) to find the realtor and Carlos-Sofia email exchange. Expected findings: (1) April 24: Brian Foster (brian.foster@mac.com) emails Carlos asking if the Torres closing is still on track for May 15, mentions two more referrals from the Torres' church group. (2) April 27: Carlos emails Sofia asking about the Torres file status and May 15 closing. (3) April 27: Sofia replies to Carlos saying conditions are in progress, 2 of 4 cleared (4506-C and pay stub), still working on bank statements and LOE, should be okay for May 15 if remaining conditions cleared this week. Discovery: Sofia's email is technically true (2 conditions cleared) but critically omits that the file is SUSPENDED at Flagstar due to the wrong borrower's bank statement. She says "should hear back from Flagstar soon once I get those uploaded" but Flagstar has already suspended the file and she has not addressed the suspension.
OE 20: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Torres" or "Flagstar" or "document mismatch" (or similar), or browse #loan-processing (C002) using conversations_history. Expected findings: (1) Darnell's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 24 tagging Sofia, saying he was checking the Flagstar portal for one of his files and noticed they flagged something on a Torres loan under her name, looks like a document mismatch, telling her to check her Flagstar emails. (2) Sofia's DM to Carlos in D_sofia_carlos on April 27 saying she replied to his email about the Torres file, conditions are in progress, two cleared already, should be good for the May 15 closing.
OE 21: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find Brian Foster's notes about the Torres deal. Expected findings: Two CRM engagement notes. (1) April 23: Brian Foster spoke with Carlos, everything seems on track for May 15 closing, Torres are excited, two more referrals from their church community. Title: "Torres purchase - on track per Carlos." (2) April 27: Brian Foster left voicemail for Carlos, emailed Friday asking about May 15 date, has not heard back. Seller attorney called asking to confirm date, Brian had to tell them he was waiting on an answer from Keystone. Says if they lose this deal it will be hard to keep sending referrals. Title: "Torres purchase - no response from Carlos, seller attorney asking."
OE 22: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Estrada" or "LN-2026-00701" (or similar) to find financial records for the Estrada file. Expected findings: Two charges. (1) Appraisal fee: $450.00 (45000 cents), charged April 14, description "Appraisal fee - Estrada (LN-2026-00701)". (2) Credit report: $65.00 (6500 cents), charged March 12, description "Credit report - Estrada (LN-2026-00701)".
OE 23: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Watkins" or "LN-2026-00702" (or similar) to find financial records for the Watkins file. Expected findings: Two charges. (1) Credit report: $65.00, charged March 10. (2) Application deposit: $400.00, charged March 12.
OE 24: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Torres" or "LN-2026-00703" (or similar) to find financial records for the Torres file. Expected findings: Two charges. (1) Credit report: $75.00, charged April 10. (2) Appraisal fee: $525.00, charged April 13.
OE 25: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (or similar) to find vendor bills related to the three loan files. Expected findings: (1) Carolina Appraisal Group bill for $450.00, doc number CAG-2026-0701, dated 2026-04-20, for appraisal at 3847 Sardis Rd (LN-2026-00701, Estrada). (2) Ballantyne Appraisal Services bill for $525.00, doc number BA-2026-0703, dated 2026-04-17, for appraisal at 8401 Pineville-Matthews Rd (LN-2026-00703, Torres). (3) CoreLogic Credco bill for $75.00, doc number CLC-2026-0703, dated 2026-04-10, for tri-merge credit report for Torres.
OE 26: Add activity log notes to the Estrada loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00701, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing the current status: appraisal gap of $20,000 ($265,000 appraised vs $285,000 purchase), property in FEMA flood zone AE requiring NFIP flood insurance adding approximately $150/month which may push DTI above FHA 50% maximum, homeowners insurance binder still pending from SouthPark Insurance, seller threatening backup offer with May 1 deadline per realtor Maria Gonzalez, borrowers do not have $20K in reserves for the gap, options include ROV or price renegotiation.
OE 27: Add activity log notes to the Watkins loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00702, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing the current status: rate lock at 6.625% expired April 27 (yesterday), current market rate approximately 7.0% representing a 37.5 bps increase and approximately $85/month higher payment, a complimentary 7-day extension was available via AmeriHome promo code SPRING2026 but was not requested before expiration, post-expiration extension may cost $500 and is not guaranteed, VOE for Angela Watkins at Novant Health remains outstanding after three failed attempts, file cannot be submitted to underwriting until VOE is completed, seller reviewing backup offers with deadline of April 30.
OE 28: Add activity log notes to the Torres loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00703, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing the current status: file SUSPENDED at Flagstar Wholesale due to wrong borrower's bank statement uploaded on 04/24 (statement belongs to Patricia Lincoln, not Priscilla Torres), 2 of 4 conditions cleared (4506-C and pay stub), bank statement condition rejected, LOE for Capital One credit inquiry still outstanding, May 15 closing date is at serious risk, correct bank statements for Priscilla and Jonathan Torres need to be obtained and uploaded immediately.
OE 29: Send an email using send_email with sender carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and recipients brian.foster@mac.com, providing Brian Foster with an honest update on the Torres closing timeline. The email should acknowledge the delay in responding, explain that the file has encountered underwriting issues at Flagstar that need to be resolved before the closing can proceed, indicate that the May 15 closing date is at risk and may need to be pushed back, and commit to providing a revised timeline once the issues are cleared. The email should NOT relay Sofia's misleading "everything is on track" message but instead reflect the actual suspended status discovered through investigation.
OE 30: Send an email using send_email (or reply_to_email to Angela Watkins' original email) with sender carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and recipients angela.watkins@outlook.com, providing Angela Watkins with an honest update on her loan file. The email should acknowledge the delay in responding, explain that the rate lock has expired and they are working on options to secure a new rate, that an employment verification is still being completed, and provide a realistic assessment of the closing timeline given the current processing delays. The email should be transparent about the challenges without being alarmist.
OE 31: Post a message in the #loan-processing Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C002" and payload summarizing the critical issues across all three of Carlos's files. The message should flag: (1) LN-2026-00701 Estrada has a $20K appraisal gap and flood zone requiring NFIP insurance with potential DTI impact, seller threatening backup offer by May 1. (2) LN-2026-00702 Watkins rate lock expired April 27, VOE still stuck at Novant Health, AmeriHome free extension promo was missed. (3) LN-2026-00703 Torres file suspended at Flagstar due to wrong borrower bank statement, correct documents needed urgently for May 15 closing. Tag or address Darnell, Sofia, and Camille so they are aware.
OE 32: Send an email using send_email with sender carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and recipients carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com (or similar, as a self-summary), providing Carlos with a consolidated summary of all three files and recommended next steps so he is prepared before Grace starts asking questions. The email should cover: (1) Estrada: appraisal gap $20K, flood insurance adds $150/month potentially pushing DTI from 44.2% above FHA 50% max, seller has backup offer deadline May 1, recommend filing ROV immediately or negotiating price reduction, need to verify DTI math with flood insurance included. (2) Watkins: rate lock expired, missed free AmeriHome SPRING2026 extension, current rate 7.0% vs locked 6.625%, contact AmeriHome about post-expiration extension ($500), escalate VOE at Novant Health (try alternative verification methods), seller deadline April 30. (3) Torres: file suspended at Flagstar due to wrong bank statement (Patricia Lincoln instead of Priscilla Torres), Sofia's update was misleading, need correct bank statements immediately, LOE for Capital One still outstanding, May 15 closing date at serious risk. Recommend discussing the document error with Sofia directly.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the mortgage LOS for the Estrada loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Estrada" (or similar), or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00701". Expected findings: FHA purchase loan for Rafael Estrada at 3847 Sardis Rd, Charlotte, NC 28270. Purchase price $285,000, loan amount $275,025, rate 6.375% locked on 2026-03-18 expiring 2026-05-02, 3.5% down payment, LTV 96.5%, DTI 44.2%, status "conditional_approval". Lender is UWM, assigned LO is Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), assigned processor is Sofia Reyes (los_staff_afc9caafae9d). Closing date 2026-05-08. Realtor is Maria Gonzalez (maria.gonzalez@icloud.com). Co-borrower is Maria Estrada. Borrower credit score 668, annual income $72,000, employer Novant Health.
OE 2: Retrieve conditions for the Estrada loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id for LN-2026-00701 (or similar). Expected findings: Two outstanding prior-to-closing conditions. Condition 1: Appraisal gap resolution required, appraised value $265,000 vs purchase price $285,000, borrower must bring additional funds or negotiate price reduction, status "outstanding", issued 2026-04-17. Condition 2: Homeowners insurance binder required, evidence of insurance must be provided to lender before closing, status "outstanding", issued 2026-03-20, binder from SouthPark Insurance pending.
OE 3: Retrieve the activity log for the Estrada loan using mortgage_los_get_activity_log with loan_id for LN-2026-00701 (or similar). Expected findings: Two notes from April 17. First by Sofia Reyes: appraisal received from Carolina Appraisal Group, value came in at $265,000, $20,000 below purchase price of $285,000. Second by Carlos Rivera: spoke with borrowers about appraisal gap, they do not have $20K in additional reserves, will look into ROV with better comps or price renegotiation with seller.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query "Estrada" or "Sardis" or "appraisal" or "LN-2026-00701" (or similar) to find email threads related to the Estrada file. Expected findings: Email from Michelle Tran at Carolina Appraisal Group (mtran@carolinaappraisal.com) to Sofia Reyes dated April 17 with the appraisal report for 3847 Sardis Rd showing appraised value $265,000, three comps used (3612 Sardis Rd $258,000, 4101 Sardis Rd $272,000, 3920 Monroe Rd $263,000), property backs up to creek with moderate erosion. CC'd to Carlos Rivera.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with query "insurance" or "binder" or "Sardis" or "flood" (or similar) to find the insurance email thread for the Estrada file. Expected findings: A 3-email chain between Tanya Morales at SouthPark Insurance (tmorales@southparkinsurance.com) and Sofia Reyes. (1) April 22: Tanya asks Sofia for the flood zone determination before issuing the binder. (2) April 23: Sofia replies with FEMA flood map info showing the property is in FEMA panel 3710578400J, rear portion of lot falls within Zone AE (1% annual chance floodplain) due to unnamed tributary of Four Mile Creek. (3) April 24: Tanya replies that because the property is in Zone AE, the borrowers will need separate NFIP flood insurance in addition to standard homeowners insurance. Breakdown: standard HOI $1,450/year, flood insurance (NFIP) $1,800/year, total $3,250/year ($270.83/month). The flood insurance alone adds approximately $150/month to the borrowers' monthly obligations. Tanya warns this may push DTI above the FHA maximum of 50%. This third email with the flood insurance breakdown is in Sofia's inbox and marked as unread (is_read = false). Carlos is not a recipient or CC on this thread, so he has no visibility into the flood insurance financial impact unless the agent reads this email chain or Sofia forwards it.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Estrada" or "insurance" or "binder" (or similar), or browse the DM channel between Sofia and Carlos (D_sofia_carlos) using conversations_history. Expected findings: Sofia's DM to Carlos on April 24 saying the insurance binder for the Estrada file is delayed, working on getting it sorted, should have an update Monday. Also Sofia's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 27 asking if anyone has dealt with Zone AE flood insurance requirement on an FHA file, mentioning it adds about $150/month and she is worried about DTI impact.
OE 7: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find realtor notes related to the Estrada deal. Expected findings: CRM engagement note dated April 22 from Maria Gonzalez regarding the Estrada purchase at 3847 Sardis Rd. The seller agent is getting very impatient with the appraisal gap situation. They have a backup offer from another buyer and are saying if the $20,000 gap is not resolved by Friday May 1 they will accept the backup offer and terminate the contract. Maria told Carlos he needs to either file the ROV immediately or get the Estradas to negotiate the price down. Title: "Estrada deal - seller threatening backup offer."
OE 8: Search the mortgage LOS for the Watkins loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Watkins" (or similar), or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00702". Expected findings: Conventional purchase loan for Angela Watkins at 6214 Fairview Rd, Charlotte, NC 28210. Purchase price $412,000, loan amount $370,800, 10% down payment, rate 6.625% locked 2026-03-13 expiring 2026-04-27 (expired yesterday), LTV 90.0%, DTI 38.5%, status "processing". Lender is AmeriHome (los_lender_533a73fed6b6), assigned LO is Carlos Rivera, assigned processor is Darnell Price (los_staff_0f9038c42665). Closing date 2026-05-22. Realtor is Derrick Coleman (derrick.coleman@icloud.com). Co-borrower Trevor Watkins. Borrower credit score 722, annual income $118,000, employer Novant Health - Presbyterian Medical Center.
OE 9: Retrieve conditions for the Watkins loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id for LN-2026-00702 (or similar). Expected findings: One outstanding prior-to-approval condition: Verbal Verification of Employment (VOE) required for Angela Watkins, employer Novant Health - Presbyterian Medical Center. Status "outstanding". Notes indicate three VOE attempts made (03/25, 04/07, 04/15), HR department at Novant Health unresponsive. File cannot be submitted to underwriting until VOE is completed.
OE 10: Check the rate lock status for the Watkins loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status with loan_id for LN-2026-00702 (or similar). Expected findings: Rate lock at 6.625% placed 2026-03-13 with AmeriHome, expired 2026-04-27 (yesterday). The lock has expired and the file is still in processing status, meaning it was never submitted to underwriting before the lock expired.
OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with query "Watkins" or "rate lock" or "LN-2026-00702" (or similar) to find the rate lock email chain. Expected findings: (1) April 20: Camille Foster warns Carlos the rate lock expires Monday April 27, lock was placed March 13 at 6.625% with AmeriHome for 45 days, file still in processing, extension fee is $375 for 7 days, needs authorization by end of day Thursday April 23. (2) April 21: Carlos replies saying he will talk to Darnell about the VOE and get back by end of week, trying to avoid the extension fee. (3) April 24: Camille escalates to Carlos CC Grace Yamamoto, saying she did not hear back, the lock expires Monday April 27, current rate for the same product is 7.0% (37.5 bps higher than 6.625%), increasing monthly payment by approximately $85. Post-expiration extension not guaranteed and costs $500 instead of $375.
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with query "AmeriHome" or "extension" or "SPRING2026" or "complimentary" (or similar) to discover the AmeriHome promotional email. Expected findings: Email dated April 23 from wholesale@amerihome.com to Darnell Price (darnell.price@keystonemortgage.com), CC'd to Sofia Reyes and Elena Marchetti, announcing a complimentary 7-day lock extension promotion. Effective through May 2, 2026, for all rate locks expiring between April 24 and May 2, 2026. Must be requested BEFORE the lock expiration date, through the AmeriHome broker portal under Lock Management, reference promo code SPRING2026. Discovery: This free extension would have covered the Watkins lock (which expired April 27, within the April 24 - May 2 window) but was never requested because the email went to Darnell, not Carlos or Camille. The opportunity is now missed since the lock already expired.
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with query "Angela Watkins" or "Fairview" or "closing timeline" (or similar) to find the borrower's email. Expected findings: Email from angela.watkins@outlook.com to Carlos dated April 27 (is_read = false), asking about the closing timeline for 6214 Fairview Rd, noting their lease ends May 31 and they were counting on closing by mid-May, the seller has been asking Derrick for updates and is getting anxious, requesting confirmation they are still on track for May 22 closing.
OE 14: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Watkins" or "rate lock" or "expir" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the Watkins file. Expected findings: (1) Camille's message in #rate-watch (C005) on April 23 listing rate locks expiring that week and next, flagging LN-2026-00702 (Watkins, conventional, AmeriHome) expires 4/27 and is still in processing, telling Carlos this needs immediate attention, also noting LN-2026-00701 (Estrada, FHA, UWM) expires 5/2 with appraisal gap unresolved. (2) Darnell's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 22 asking if anyone has a direct HR contact at Novant Health Presbyterian, noting he has been trying to complete a VOE for 5 weeks on the Watkins file (LN-2026-00702) and the main HR line just goes to voicemail after three attempts.
OE 15: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find realtor notes about the Watkins deal. Expected findings: CRM engagement note dated April 24 from Derrick Coleman regarding the Watkins purchase at 6214 Fairview Rd. The seller is reviewing a backup offer and needs a firm answer on closing timeline by end of day Wednesday April 30. The file has been in processing for over 6 weeks with no movement. This is his third time asking for an update this month. Title: "Watkins purchase - seller considering backup offers."
OE 16: Search the mortgage LOS for the Torres loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Torres" (or similar), or mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00703". Expected findings: Conventional purchase loan for Priscilla Torres at 8401 Pineville-Matthews Rd, Charlotte, NC 28226. Purchase price $348,000, loan amount $313,200, 10% down payment, rate 6.75% locked 2026-04-01 expiring 2026-05-16, LTV 90.0%, DTI 36.8%, status "underwriting". Lender is Flagstar Wholesale (los_lender_fff8b2eea94d), assigned LO is Carlos Rivera, assigned processor is Sofia Reyes. Closing date 2026-05-15. Realtor is Brian Foster (brian.foster@mac.com). Co-borrower Jonathan Torres. Borrower credit score 735, annual income $104,000, employer Wells Fargo Corporate Office.
OE 17: Retrieve conditions for the Torres loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id for LN-2026-00703 (or similar). Expected findings: Four conditions. (1) Bank statements: "rejected" status, description says provide 2 months consecutive bank statements, only 1 month received. Notes reveal that the bank statement uploaded on 04/24 was rejected by underwriter because the name on the statement does not match borrower -- statement shows Patricia Lincoln, not Priscilla Torres. File suspended pending correct document. (2) IRS Form 4506-C: "cleared" status, cleared by Sofia on 04/23. (3) Pay stub: "cleared" status, cleared by Sofia on 04/23. (4) Letter of explanation for credit inquiry from Capital One Auto Finance dated 03/15/2026: "outstanding" status, borrower has not yet provided LOE.
OE 18: Search emails using search_emails with query "Torres" or "Flagstar" or "LN-2026-00703" or "suspended" (or similar) to find the Flagstar correspondence. Expected findings: (1) April 22: Flagstar Wholesale Underwriting (submissions@flagstar.com) emails Sofia issuing 4 conditions for LN-2026-00703 (bank statements, 4506-C, pay stub, LOE for Capital One credit inquiry). (2) April 24: Flagstar emails Sofia with subject "FILE SUSPENDED" -- the bank statement uploaded on 04/24 does not match the borrower, document shows Patricia Lincoln with Chase Bank account ending 7842 and balance $48,231.56, but borrower is Priscilla Torres. File suspended until correct documents received. This email is marked as unread (is_read = false). Discovery: This is the critical evidence that Sofia's "conditions are moving along fine" update to Carlos was misleading -- she either did not see or did not disclose the file suspension.
OE 19: Search emails using search_emails with query "Torres" or "Brian Foster" or "May 15" or "Pineville" (or similar) to find the realtor and Carlos-Sofia email exchange. Expected findings: (1) April 24: Brian Foster (brian.foster@mac.com) emails Carlos asking if the Torres closing is still on track for May 15, mentions two more referrals from the Torres' church group. (2) April 27: Carlos emails Sofia asking about the Torres file status and May 15 closing. (3) April 27: Sofia replies to Carlos saying conditions are in progress, 2 of 4 cleared (4506-C and pay stub), still working on bank statements and LOE, should be okay for May 15 if remaining conditions cleared this week. Discovery: Sofia's email is technically true (2 conditions cleared) but critically omits that the file is SUSPENDED at Flagstar due to the wrong borrower's bank statement. She says "should hear back from Flagstar soon once I get those uploaded" but Flagstar has already suspended the file and she has not addressed the suspension.
OE 20: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Torres" or "Flagstar" or "document mismatch" (or similar), or browse #loan-processing (C002) using conversations_history. Expected findings: (1) Darnell's message in #loan-processing (C002) on April 24 tagging Sofia, saying he was checking the Flagstar portal for one of his files and noticed they flagged something on a Torres loan under her name, looks like a document mismatch, telling her to check her Flagstar emails. (2) Sofia's DM to Carlos in D_sofia_carlos on April 27 saying she replied to his email about the Torres file, conditions are in progress, two cleared already, should be good for the May 15 closing.
OE 21: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find Brian Foster's notes about the Torres deal. Expected findings: Two CRM engagement notes. (1) April 23: Brian Foster spoke with Carlos, everything seems on track for May 15 closing, Torres are excited, two more referrals from their church community. Title: "Torres purchase - on track per Carlos." (2) April 27: Brian Foster left voicemail for Carlos, emailed Friday asking about May 15 date, has not heard back. Seller attorney called asking to confirm date, Brian had to tell them he was waiting on an answer from Keystone. Says if they lose this deal it will be hard to keep sending referrals. Title: "Torres purchase - no response from Carlos, seller attorney asking."
OE 22: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Estrada" or "LN-2026-00701" (or similar) to find financial records for the Estrada file. Expected findings: Two charges. (1) Appraisal fee: $450.00 (45000 cents), charged April 14, description "Appraisal fee - Estrada (LN-2026-00701)". (2) Credit report: $65.00 (6500 cents), charged March 12, description "Credit report - Estrada (LN-2026-00701)".
OE 23: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Watkins" or "LN-2026-00702" (or similar) to find financial records for the Watkins file. Expected findings: Two charges. (1) Credit report: $65.00, charged March 10. (2) Application deposit: $400.00, charged March 12.
OE 24: Search Stripe charges using stripe_search_charges with query "Torres" or "LN-2026-00703" (or similar) to find financial records for the Torres file. Expected findings: Two charges. (1) Credit report: $75.00, charged April 10. (2) Appraisal fee: $525.00, charged April 13.
OE 25: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (or similar) to find vendor bills related to the three loan files. Expected findings: (1) Carolina Appraisal Group bill for $450.00, doc number CAG-2026-0701, dated 2026-04-20, for appraisal at 3847 Sardis Rd (LN-2026-00701, Estrada). (2) Ballantyne Appraisal Services bill for $525.00, doc number BA-2026-0703, dated 2026-04-17, for appraisal at 8401 Pineville-Matthews Rd (LN-2026-00703, Torres). (3) CoreLogic Credco bill for $75.00, doc number CLC-2026-0703, dated 2026-04-10, for tri-merge credit report for Torres.
OE 26: Add activity log notes to the Estrada loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00701, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing the current status: appraisal gap of $20,000 ($265,000 appraised vs $285,000 purchase), property in FEMA flood zone AE requiring NFIP flood insurance adding approximately $150/month which may push DTI above FHA 50% maximum, homeowners insurance binder still pending from SouthPark Insurance, seller threatening backup offer with May 1 deadline per realtor Maria Gonzalez, borrowers do not have $20K in reserves for the gap, options include ROV or price renegotiation.
OE 27: Add activity log notes to the Watkins loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00702, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing the current status: rate lock at 6.625% expired April 27 (yesterday), current market rate approximately 7.0% representing a 37.5 bps increase and approximately $85/month higher payment, a complimentary 7-day extension was available via AmeriHome promo code SPRING2026 but was not requested before expiration, post-expiration extension may cost $500 and is not guaranteed, VOE for Angela Watkins at Novant Health remains outstanding after three failed attempts, file cannot be submitted to underwriting until VOE is completed, seller reviewing backup offers with deadline of April 30.
OE 28: Add activity log notes to the Torres loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2026-00703, action "note" (or similar), and detail summarizing the current status: file SUSPENDED at Flagstar Wholesale due to wrong borrower's bank statement uploaded on 04/24 (statement belongs to Patricia Lincoln, not Priscilla Torres), 2 of 4 conditions cleared (4506-C and pay stub), bank statement condition rejected, LOE for Capital One credit inquiry still outstanding, May 15 closing date is at serious risk, correct bank statements for Priscilla and Jonathan Torres need to be obtained and uploaded immediately.
OE 29: Send an email using send_email with sender carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and recipients brian.foster@mac.com, providing Brian Foster with an honest update on the Torres closing timeline. The email should acknowledge the delay in responding, explain that the file has encountered underwriting issues at Flagstar that need to be resolved before the closing can proceed, indicate that the May 15 closing date is at risk and may need to be pushed back, and commit to providing a revised timeline once the issues are cleared. The email should NOT relay Sofia's misleading "everything is on track" message but instead reflect the actual suspended status discovered through investigation.
OE 30: Send an email using send_email (or reply_to_email to Angela Watkins' original email) with sender carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and recipients angela.watkins@outlook.com, providing Angela Watkins with an honest update on her loan file. The email should acknowledge the delay in responding, explain that the rate lock has expired and they are working on options to secure a new rate, that an employment verification is still being completed, and provide a realistic assessment of the closing timeline given the current processing delays. The email should be transparent about the challenges without being alarmist.
OE 31: Post a message in the #loan-processing Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C002" and payload summarizing the critical issues across all three of Carlos's files. The message should flag: (1) LN-2026-00701 Estrada has a $20K appraisal gap and flood zone requiring NFIP insurance with potential DTI impact, seller threatening backup offer by May 1. (2) LN-2026-00702 Watkins rate lock expired April 27, VOE still stuck at Novant Health, AmeriHome free extension promo was missed. (3) LN-2026-00703 Torres file suspended at Flagstar due to wrong borrower bank statement, correct documents needed urgently for May 15 closing. Tag or address Darnell, Sofia, and Camille so they are aware.
OE 32: Send an email using send_email with sender carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com and recipients carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com (or similar, as a self-summary), providing Carlos with a consolidated summary of all three files and recommended next steps so he is prepared before Grace starts asking questions. The email should cover: (1) Estrada: appraisal gap $20K, flood insurance adds $150/month potentially pushing DTI from 44.2% above FHA 50% max, seller has backup offer deadline May 1, recommend filing ROV immediately or negotiating price reduction, need to verify DTI math with flood insurance included. (2) Watkins: rate lock expired, missed free AmeriHome SPRING2026 extension, current rate 7.0% vs locked 6.625%, contact AmeriHome about post-expiration extension ($500), escalate VOE at Novant Health (try alternative verification methods), seller deadline April 30. (3) Torres: file suspended at Flagstar due to wrong bank statement (Patricia Lincoln instead of Priscilla Torres), Sofia's update was misleading, need correct bank statements immediately, LOE for Capital One still outstanding, May 15 closing date at serious risk. Recommend discussing the document error with Sofia directly.

1 An activity note was added to the Estrada loan (LN-2026-00701) using mortgage_los_add_activity (or similar) with the loan_id for LN-2026-00701.
outcome

✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'LN-2026-00701' for the Estrada file (tried with action 'File Review' first, then retried with action 'note'). The tool call was made with the correct loan_id.
✓Run #2The agent used filesystem_write_file to create 'documents/LN-2026-00701_Estrada_activity_note_20260428.txt', which constitutes a similar activity note mechanism tied to loan LN-2026-00701. The criterion allows for '(or similar)' tools.
✓Run #3Tool Call 72 used filesystem_write_file to save activity notes to 'documents/LN-2026-00701_Estrada/activity_notes_2026-04-28.txt'. While it used filesystem_write_file instead of mortgage_los_add_activity, the '(or similar)' clause covers this and the loan_id LN-2026-00701 is embedded in the file path.
✓Run #4The agent used filesystem_write_file (path: documents/LN-2026-00701_Estrada) to create a detailed activity log for the Estrada loan. While not mortgage_los_add_activity, this qualifies as 'similar' given the explicit loan ID in the path and the full paper trail content.
✗Run #5No mortgage_los_add_activity tool call was found in the trajectory for LN-2026-00701. The agent marked the task as 'completed' in TodoWrite but never actually called any activity-note tool on the LOS. The LOS get_loan calls all returned 'not found' errors and the agent moved on without adding notes.
✗Run #6The agent attempted mortgage_los_add_activity for LN-2026-00701 twice (once with action 'File Review' which was rejected, then with action 'note') but both calls returned 'Loan not found' errors. The note was not actually added to the LOS. The agent instead saved a filesystem file as a workaround, which is not the same as an LOS activity note.

outcome

outcome

11 The email sent to Brian Foster ([brian.foster@mac.com](mailto:brian.foster@mac.com)) indicates that the May 15 closing date is at risk or may need to be pushed back due to underwriting issues at Flagstar, rather than relaying Sofia's misleading "everything is on track" message.
outcome

It does not claim the closing is on track, instead flagging the suspension issue.
✓Run #2The email to Brian Foster states: 'As for the May 15 closing date: it is tight but still achievable if... I'd suggest telling them we are targeting May 15 but to hold flexibility for a possible short extension to the week of May 18 as a contingency.' It does not say everything is on track, and explicitly signals risk with a contingency plan. The email does not relay Sofia's misleading 'on track' message.
✓Run #3Tool Call 76's email to Brian Foster states the May 15 date is 'tight but possible' with a more realistic target of May 19-22, and commits to a firm answer by Wednesday. This communicates the closing is at risk and does not say everything is on track.
✓Run #4The email to Brian Foster says May 15 is 'tight but possible' with a backup of May 19-22, and references document upload errors being corrected and remaining conditions — accurately reflecting the suspended/at-risk status rather than confirming the closing is on track.
✓Run #5The email to Brian Foster explicitly states 'The May 15 closing date is now at risk' and explains the Flagstar suspension and document error. It does NOT claim the closing is on track.
✓Run #6The email to Brian Foster clearly states 'the May 15 closing date is tight' and 'realistically we may need to look at May 20-22 depending on how quickly Flagstar turns things around,' targeting the 'week of May 18-22 as our realistic closing window.' It does NOT say the closing is on track.

✓Run #6The summary email provides the complete insurance cost breakdown: 'Standard homeowners: $1,450/yr,' 'NFIP flood insurance: $1,800/yr,' and 'TOTAL: $3,250/yr = $270.83/month,' satisfying all three required figures.

26 The agent's final response or the summary email to Carlos correctly identifies that Sofia's update on the Torres file was misleading or incomplete: while she reported 2 of 4 conditions cleared and said things "should be okay for May 15," the file was actually suspended at Flagstar.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The summary email explicitly states: 'Sofia told you yesterday that conditions are in progress, should be good for May 15 closing. This is WRONG.' and documents that Flagstar suspended the file. Both the misleading update and the actual suspension are clearly identified.
✓Run #2The final response states: 'Sofia's update was wrong. The file is SUSPENDED.' The summary email states: 'Sofia told you 2 of 4 conditions cleared, should be good for May 15. THIS IS NOT ACCURATE. The file is SUSPENDED at Flagstar.' The contradiction between Sofia's update and the actual suspended file status is clearly identified.
✓Run #3The agent's final response explicitly states 'Sofia's update was wrong. She said 2 of 4 conditions cleared... Reality: Flagstar SUSPENDED the file on 4/22.' The contradiction is clearly identified.
✓Run #4The agent explicitly identifies that Sofia's update ('conditions are in progress... should be good for May 15') was contradicted by the Flagstar suspension, noting the suspension email appeared unread by Sofia. This contradiction is flagged prominently in the response, Slack message, and Torres activity note.
✓Run #5The agent clearly identifies the contradiction: Sofia said on 4/25 'conditions are in progress, two cleared already. should be good for the may 15 closing' while the file was actually SUSPENDED by Flagstar on 4/24 — the day before her update.
✓Run #6The agent explicitly identifies Sofia's update as inaccurate in both the final response ('Sofia's update was not accurate') and the summary email ('Sofia told you conditions are on track, but the file was suspended at Flagstar on 4/24'), directly connecting her 'conditions moving along fine' claim to the reality of the suspended file.
